props.name: button
props.docs: Displays the summary as a button and hides the default marker.

props.name: buttonColor
props.docs: The color of the button. Only applies if `button` is `true`.

props.name: buttonIconClose
props.docs: BalIcon of the close trigger button

props.name: buttonIconOpen
props.docs: BalIcon of the open trigger button

props.name: buttonLabelClose
props.docs: Label of the close trigger button

props.name: buttonLabelOpen
props.docs: Label of the open trigger button

props.name: buttonSize
props.docs: The size of the button. Only applies if `button` is `true`.

props.name: buttonWide
props.docs: If `true` the button is expanded to full width. Only applies if `button` is `true`.

props.name: group
props.docs:
The name of the group the accordion belongs to. Accordions with the same group name will automatically
close when another accordion in the same group is opened.

props.name: marker
props.docs:
The marker variant. Only applies if `button` is `false`.
If `''` the default marker is used, if `plus` a plus icon is used and if `plus-minus`
a plus icon for closed and a minus icon for open state is used.

props.name: markerPosition
props.docs: The position of the marker. Only applies if `button` is `false`.

props.name: open
props.docs: If `true` the accordion is open.

props.name: summaryLevel
props.docs: The heading level of the summary

props.name: summaryTitle
props.docs: If `true` the summary is styled as a title.

props.name: summaryVisualLevel
props.docs: The visual heading level of the summary.

props.name: animated
props.docs: Disables all animation inside the ds-app. Can be used for simplify e2e testing.

events.docs: Emitted when app is ready and painted.

props.name: color
props.docs: Define the color for the badge.

props.name: icon
props.docs: Name of the icon to show. If a icon is present text should be hidden.

props.name: position
props.docs: If `true` the badge is added to the top right corner of the card.

props.name: pulse
props.docs: If `true` the badge is added to the top right corner of the card.

props.name: size
props.docs: Define the size of badge. Small is recommended for tabs.

props.name: a11yControls
props.docs: A11y attributes for the native button element.

props.name: a11yHaspopup
props.docs: A11y attributes for the native button element.

props.name: a11yLabel
props.docs: A11y attributes for the native button element.

props.name: a11yTitle
props.docs: A11y attributes for the native button element.

props.name: circle
props.docs: If `true` the button is circular and width of the buttons is limited

props.name: color
props.docs: The color to use from your application's color palette.aaa

props.name: dashed
props.docs: If `true` the button has a dashed border.

props.name: disabled
props.docs: If `true`, the user cannot interact with the button.

props.name: download
props.docs:
This attribute instructs browsers to download a URL instead of navigating to
it, so the user will be prompted to save it as a local file. If the attribute
has a value, it is used as the pre-filled file name in the Save prompt
(the user can still change the file name if they want).

props.name: elementType
props.docs: The type of button.

props.name: flat
props.docs: If `true` the button has no padding and a reduced height

props.name: href
props.docs: Specifies the URL of the page the link goes to

props.name: icon
props.docs: Name of the left button icon

props.name: iconRight
props.docs: Name of the right button icon

props.name: iconTurn
props.docs: If `true` the icon turns

props.name: inverted
props.docs: If `true` the button is inverted

props.name: loading
props.docs: If `true` the label is hidden and a loading spinner is shown instead.

props.name: name
props.docs: The name of the button, which is submitted with the form data.

props.name: noWrap
props.docs: The label of the button will not break

props.name: outlined
props.docs: If `true` the button is outlined

props.name: rel
props.docs:
Specifies the relationship of the target object to the link object.
The value is a space-separated list of [link types](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Link_types).

props.name: rounded
props.docs: If `true` the button is rounded.

props.name: shadow
props.docs: If `true` adds a box shadow to improve readability on image background

props.name: size
props.docs: Size of the button

props.name: square
props.docs: If `true` the width of the buttons is limited

props.name: target
props.docs:
Specifies where to display the linked URL.
Only applies when an `href` is provided.

props.name: value
props.docs: The value of the button, which is submitted with the form data.

props.name: wide
props.docs: If `true` the button has a full width

props.name: align
props.docs: The value of the button, which is submitted with the form data.

props.name: direction
props.docs:
`auto` will position the button items vertical and full width.
`row` will force that the buttons are also horizontal on mobile.

props.name: reverse
props.docs: If `true` the flex direction is used in reverse on mobile.

props.name: wide
props.docs: If `true` the buttons will expand to fill the available space on mobile.

props.name: align
props.docs: Defines the text alignment of the card content.

props.name: clickable
props.docs: If `true` the card has a hover effect.

props.docs: Defines the color of the card.

props.name: dense
props.docs: If `true` the card gets a smaller padding.

props.name: flat
props.docs: If `true` the card loses its shadow.

props.name: fullheight
props.docs: If `true` the card uses 100% of the available height.

props.name: imageTeaser
props.docs:
If `true` the card image is displayed as a teaser, which means
it is displayed with a large image.

props.name: inverted
props.docs: If `true` the card background color becomes blue.

props.name: outlined
props.docs: If `true` the cards gets a light border and loses its shadow.

props.name: selected
props.docs: If `true` the card gets a light background to indicate a selection.

props.name: space
props.docs: Defines the space of the card content.

props.name: square
props.docs: If `true` the card loses its border radius.

props.name: tile
props.docs: If `true` the card gets a tile look, it has a brand icon on the left

props.name: visualLevel
props.docs:
Make the visual style mimic a specific heading level.
This option allows you to make e.g. h1 visually look like h3,
but still keep it h1 in the markup.

props.docs: If `true`, the checkbox is selected.

props.name: disabled
props.docs: If `true`, the element is not mutable, focusable, or even submitted with the form. The user can neither edit nor focus on the control, nor its form control descendants.

props.name: invalid
props.docs: If `true` the component gets a invalid style.

props.name: label
props.docs: Label of the radio item.

props.docs: Defines the position of the label, either before or after the radio input. Default is after.

props.name: name
props.docs: The name of the control, which is submitted with the form data.

props.docs: If `true` the element can not mutated, meaning the user can not edit the control.

A DOMString representing the value of the checkbox. This is not displayed on the
client-side, but on the server this is the value given to the data
submitted with the checkbox's name.

props.name: button
props.docs:
If `true` the close component will be rendered as a button element.
This is useful when you want to use the close component outside of a notification or alert, for example as a standalone button.

props.name: buttonColor
props.docs: Defines the color of the button variant. Only applicable if `button` is `true`.

props.name: disabled
props.docs: If `true` the close component will be disabled and not interactive.

props.name: inverted
props.docs: If `true` it supports dark backgrounds.

props.name: size
props.docs: Define the size of badge. Small is recommended for tabs.

props.name: align
props.docs:
Defines the positioning like center, end or
default to start.

props.name: direction
props.docs: Defines the direction of the child elements. Default is column.

docsTags.name: deprecated
docsTags.text:
Please use direction instead.
Defines the position of the child elements if they
are showed verticaly or horizontally. Default is horizontally.

Please use direction instead.

are showed verticaly or horizontally. Default is horizontally.

props.name: space

Defines the text positioning like center, right or
default to left.

props.name: dashed

Defines the position of the child elements if they
are showed verticaly or horizontally. Default is verticaly.

props.docs: Defines the space between the child elements. Default is xx-small.

props.name: noWrap
props.docs:
When true, the text will be truncated with a text overflow ellipsis instead of wrapping.
Please note that text overflow can only occur in block or inline-block level elements,
as these elements require a width to overflow.

props.name: shadow
props.docs: If `true` adds a text shadow to improve readability on image background

props.name: space
props.docs: Defines at which position the heading has spacing.

props.name: subtitle
props.docs: If `true` the heading gets displayed slimmer.

props.name: visualLevel
props.docs:
Make the visual style mimic a specific heading level.
This option allows you to make e.g. h1 visually look like h3,
but still keep it h1 in the markup.

props.name: color
props.docs: The theme type of the button.

props.name: disabled
props.docs: If `true`, the element is not mutable, focusable, or even submitted with the form. The user can neither edit nor focus on the control, nor its form control descendants.

props.name: inline
props.docs: If `true` the icon has display inline style

props.name: invalid
props.docs: If `true` the component gets a invalid red style.

props.name: inverted
props.docs: If `true` the icon is inverted

props.name: name
props.docs: Name of the baloise icon.

props.name: shadow
props.docs: If `true` adds a box shadow to improve readability on image background

props.name: shape
props.docs: If `true` the icon is displayed in a circle with a background color.

props.name: size
props.docs: Defines the size of the icon.

props.name: svg
props.docs: Svg content.

props.name: tile
props.docs: If `true` the icon acts as a tile with a background color.

props.name: tileColor
props.docs: If `true` the icon acts as a tile with a background color. Default is purple

props.name: turn
props.docs: If `true` the icon is rotated 180deg

props.name: accept
props.docs: If the value of the type attribute is `"file"`, then this attribute will indicate the types of files that the server accepts, otherwise it will be ignored. The value must be a comma-separated list of unique content type specifiers.

props.name: allowedKeyPress
props.docs: A regular expression that the key of the key press event is checked against and if not matching the expression the event will be prevented.

props.name: autoInvalidOff
props.docs: If `true`, in Angular reactive forms the control will not be set invalid

props.name: autocapitalize
props.docs:
Indicates whether and how the text value should be automatically capitalized as it is entered/edited by the user.
Available options: `"off"`, `"none"`, `"on"`, `"sentences"`, `"words"`, `"characters"`.

props.name: autocomplete
props.docs: Indicates whether the value of the control can be automatically completed by the browser.

props.name: autocorrect
props.docs: Whether auto correction should be enabled when the user is entering/editing the text value.

props.name: autofocus
props.docs: This Boolean attribute lets you specify that a form control should have input focus when the page loads.

props.name: color
props.docs: Defines the color of the input. The default value is `primary`.

props.name: debounce
props.docs: Set the amount of time, in milliseconds, to wait to trigger the `dsChange` event after each keystroke. This also impacts form bindings such as `ngModel` or `v-model`.

props.name: description
props.docs: The description of the input, which is displayed below the input field.

props.name: disabled
props.docs: If `true`, the element is not mutable, focusable, or even submitted with the form. The user can neither edit nor focus on the control, nor its form control descendants.

props.name: inputmode
props.docs:
A hint to the browser for which keyboard to display.
Possible values: `"none"`, `"text"`, `"tel"`, `"url"`,
`"email"`, `"numeric"`, `"decimal"`, and `"search"`.

props.name: invalid
props.docs: If `true` the component gets a invalid style.

props.name: invalidText
props.docs: The text to display when the input is in an invalid state.

props.name: label
props.docs: The label of the input, which is displayed above the input field.

props.name: loading
props.docs: Shows a loading indicator at the end of the input and replaces the end slot content.

props.name: mask
props.docs:
Mask of the input field. It defines what the user can enter and how the format looks like. Currently, only for Switzerland formatted with addition of Belgian enterprisenumber and IBAN.
Formatting for 'contract-number': '99/1.234.567-1'
Formatting for 'basic-contract-number': '99/1.234.567'
Formatting for 'claim-number': ('73/001217/16.9')
Formatting for 'offer-number': ('98/7.654.321')
Formatting for 'be-enterprise-number': ('1234.567.890')
Formatting for 'be-iban': ('BE68 5390 0754 7034')

props.name: max
props.docs: The maximum value, which must not be less than its minimum (min attribute) value.

props.name: maxLength
props.docs: Defines the max length of the value.

props.name: min
props.docs: The minimum value, which must not be greater than its maximum (max attribute) value.

props.name: minLength
props.docs: Defines the min length of the value.

props.name: multiple
props.docs: If `true`, the user can enter more than one value. This attribute applies when the type attribute is set to `"email"` or `"file"`, otherwise it is ignored.

props.name: name
props.docs: The name of the control, which is submitted with the form data.

props.name: pattern
props.docs: A regular expression that the value is checked against. The pattern must match the entire value, not just some subset. Use the title attribute to describe the pattern to help the user. This attribute applies when the value of the type attribute is `"text"`, `"search"`, `"tel"`, `"url"`, `"email"`, `"date"`, or `"password"`, otherwise it is ignored. When the type attribute is `"date"`, `pattern` will only be used in browsers that do not support the `"date"` input type natively. See https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input/date for more information.

props.name: placeholder
props.docs: Instructional text that shows before the input has a value.

props.name: readonly
props.docs: If `true` the element can not mutated, meaning the user can not edit the control.

props.name: required
props.docs: If `true`, the user must fill in a value before submitting a form.

props.name: spellcheck
props.docs: If `true`, the element will have its spelling and grammar checked.

props.name: suffix
props.docs: Adds a suffix the the input-value after blur.

props.name: type
props.docs: Defines the type of the input (text, number, email ...).

props.name: value
props.docs: The value of the input.

methods.name: getInputElement
methods.docs: Returns the native `<input>` element used under the hood.

methods.name: setFocus
methods.docs:
Sets focus on the native `input` in `ds-input`. Use this method instead of the global
`input.focus()`.

events.docs: Emitted when a keyboard input occurred.
Emitted when the input value has changed.

props.name: accordionGroup
props.docs:
The name of the group the accordion belongs to. Accordions with the same group name will automatically
close when another accordion in the same group is opened.

props.name: accordionMarker
props.docs:
The marker variant. Only applies if `button` is `false`.
If `''` the default marker is used, if `plus` a plus icon is used and if `plus-minus`
a plus icon for closed and a minus icon for open state is used.

props.name: accordionMarkerPosition
props.docs: The position of the marker. Only applies if `button` is `false`.

props.name: accordionOpen
props.docs: If `true` the accordion is open.

props.name: disabled
props.docs: If `true`, the user cannot interact with the button.

props.name: download
props.docs:
This attribute instructs browsers to download a URL instead of navigating to
it, so the user will be prompted to save it as a local file. If the attribute
has a value, it is used as the pre-filled file name in the Save prompt
(the user can still change the file name if they want).

props.name: href
props.docs: Specifies the URL of the page the link goes to

props.name: rel
props.docs:
Specifies the relationship of the target object to the link object.
The value is a space-separated list of [link types](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Link_types).

props.name: target
props.docs:
Specifies where to display the linked URL.
Only applies when an `href` is provided.

props.name: htmlFor
props.docs:
The value of the for attribute must be a single id for a labeled
form-related element in the same document as the <label> element.
So, any given label element can be associated with only one form control.

props.name: htmlId
props.docs: Define the id of the native label element

props.name: invalid
props.docs: If `true` the component gets a invalid red style.

props.name: noWrap
props.docs:
When true, the text will be truncated with a text overflow ellipsis instead of wrapping.
Please note that text overflow can only occur in block or inline-block level elements,
as these elements require a width to overflow.

props.name: required
props.docs:
If `true` the form control needs to be filled. If it is set to
`false` an optional label is added to the label..

props.name: size
props.docs:
Defines the size of the font. Default is like a heading 5 and small is used
with the form fields.

props.name: valid
props.docs: If `true` the component gets a valid green style.

Defines the color of the element
Color type primary is deprecated, please use info instead.

props.name: decimal
props.docs: Number of allowed decimal places. `0` means integers only.

props.name: description
props.docs: The description displayed below the field.

props.name: disabled
props.docs: If `true`, the element is not mutable, focusable, or submitted with the form.

props.docs: When `true`, displays `0` instead of an empty field when value is null.

props.name: invalid
props.docs: If `true` the component gets an invalid style.

props.name: invalidText

props.name: label
props.docs: The label displayed above the field.

props.name: max
props.docs: The maximum value.

props.name: min
props.docs: The minimum value.

props.name: name
props.docs: The name of the control, which is submitted with the form data.

props.name: onlyPositive
props.docs: When `true`, only positive numbers are accepted (blocks the minus sign).

props.name: pattern
props.docs: Overrides the auto-generated input validation pattern.

props.name: placeholder
props.docs: Instructional text shown when the input has no value.

props.name: readonly
props.docs: If `true`, the element cannot be edited by the user.

props.name: required
props.docs: If `true`, the user must fill in a value before submitting a form.

props.name: suffix
props.docs: Text appended to the formatted value after blur (e.g. `"CHF"`).

props.name: value
props.docs: The numeric value of the input. `null` means no value.

props.name: noWrap
props.docs:
When true, the text will be truncated with a text overflow ellipsis instead of wrapping.
Please note that text overflow can only occur in block or inline-block level elements,
as these elements require a width to overflow.

props.docs: If `true` adds a text shadow to improve readability on image background

props.docs: The number of visible text lines for the control.
